etymology.description: semantic 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic 剌(剌) (là) -> sound production through a mouth -> horn sound
etymology.interpretation: The character 喇(lǎ, "horn; sound of a trumpet") combines 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth"), which signifies sound production or speaking, with 剌(剌) (là) as the phonetic component, providing the pronunciation. This combination evokes the imagery of the mouth producing sound, akin to the sound made by a horn or trumpet.

examples.chinese: 他在演奏喇叭(他在演奏喇叭)。
马路上车辆喇叭声很大(馬路上車輛喇叭聲很大)。
examples.pinyin: Mǎlù shàng chēliàng lǎbā shēng hěn dà.
examples.english: The sound of car horns is loud on the street.

examples.chinese: 他们在庆典上吹喇叭(他們在慶典上吹喇叭)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen zài qìngdiǎn shàng chuī lǎbā.
examples.english: They are blowing horns at the celebration.
